dismissalText.commentary: Mitchell Marsh c Stubbs b Kuldeep Yadav 35 (28b 2x4 3x6 53m) SR: 125

dismissalComment.html: Kuldeep's been floating it up, has taken a few big hits... and now he bears fruit! It's slow as ever through the air, and just spins away from the batter and is closer to him than Marsh might have accounted for. It was a googly, too. Marsh's bat twists in his hand and he mistimes this in the air. Stubbs pouches this at mid-off

dismissalText.commentary: Rishabh Pant run out 7 (9b 1x4 0x6 16m) SR: 77.77

dismissalComment.html: MUKESH IS RUNNING OFF, Tahir-style! What's happened here!? Marsh drills a drive off a good length straight down the pitch. Too straight, in fact, because Mukesh gets a hand on it and the ball ricochets into the stumps. Pant is out of his crease, and what an unfortunate way to be dismissed...

dismissalText.commentary: Nicholas Pooran b Ngidi 8 (8b 1x4 0x6 11m) SR: 100

dismissalComment.html: That's almost vintage Bravo, but somehow better! Ball of the tournament so far, really. Pooran goes on the front foot, trying to play this full ball down into the off-side. But Ngidi has disguised his off-cutter to perfection. It floats through the air, impossibly slow, and somehow, sneaks past Pooran's bat and rattles his stumps. Magical T20 bowling, this

dismissalText.commentary: Abdul Samad c Miller b Natarajan 36 (25b 3x4 1x6 43m) SR: 144

dismissalComment.html: That rockets... straight to Miller at long-off! That was travelling at pace, very flat, Miller must've been taken aback. He was a few feet in the ropes, was leaning back as the ball came, then took it reverse-cupped. Took a step or two back as he got carried by the momentum. Was a slot ball in the hitting range, Samad drove it hard with a full follow-through
